ddls:
CREATE TABLE "daily_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the report */,
  "confirmed" INTEGER /* Total confirmed cases */,
  "deaths" INTEGER /* Total deaths */,
  "recovered" INTEGER /* Total recovered cases */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Total active cases */,
  "new_cases" INTEGER /* New cases reported on this date */,
  "deaths_per_100_cases" REAL /* Deaths per 100 cases */,
  "recovered_per_100_cases" REAL /* Recovered cases per 100 cases */,
  "deaths_per_100_recovered" REAL /* Deaths per 100 recovered cases */,
  "new_deaths" INTEGER /* Number of new deaths reported on this date */,
  "new_recoveries" INTEGER /* Number of new recoveries reported on this date */,
  "testing_rate" REAL /* Number of tests conducted per 100,000 people */,
  "vaccination_rate" REAL /* Percentage of the population vaccinated */,
  "countries_affected" INTEGER /* Number of countries affected */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source for this report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who contributed this report */,
  "validated" INTEGER /* Boolean flag to indicate if the report has been validated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_daily_reports_countries_affected FOREIGN KEY ("countries_affected") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_daily_reports_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_daily_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code of the country */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the country */,
  "area" INTEGER /* Total area of the country in square kilometers */,
  "capital" TEXT /* Capital city of the country */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Official currency of the country */,
  "flag_url" TEXT /* URL to the flag of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  "reliability_score" INTEGER /* Reliability score of the data source (1-10) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data contributor, analyst, administrator) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean flag to indicate if the user account is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analysis */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the analysis */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the analysis */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the analysis was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the analysis was last updated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analysis_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
column_names: New cases reported on this date
